subject: science
question: What do plants need to grow?
explanation: Plants need water and sunlight to grow! They drink water through their roots and soak up sunshine through their leaves.

subject: science
question: What is a baby dog called?
explanation: A baby dog is called a puppy! Puppies are so cute and playful!

subject: science
question: Which body part do we use to see?
explanation: We use our eyes to see! Eyes help us look at all the beautiful things around us.

subject: science
question: What falls from the clouds when it rains?
explanation: Water falls from the clouds as rain! Clouds hold tiny drops of water that fall down when they get heavy.

subject: science
question: How many legs does a spider have?
explanation: A spider has 8 legs! That is a lot of legs! Spiders are not insects -- they are arachnids.

subject: science
question: What is the biggest star we can see during the day?
explanation: The Sun is a star! It is the closest star to Earth and gives us light and warmth.

subject: science
question: Which sense do we use our nose for?
explanation: We use our nose for smelling! Your nose can smell flowers, food, and so many things!

subject: science
question: What is a baby cat called?
explanation: A baby cat is called a kitten! Kittens love to play with yarn!

subject: science
question: In which season do leaves fall from trees?
explanation: Leaves fall in autumn (also called fall)! They turn beautiful red, orange, and yellow colors.

subject: science
question: Which animal lives in water?
explanation: Fish live in water! They breathe through special parts called gills.

subject: science
question: What part of a plant grows under the ground?
explanation: Roots grow under the ground! They hold the plant in place and drink up water from the soil.

subject: science
question: What do we breathe in?
explanation: We breathe in air! Air has oxygen, which our body needs to stay alive.

subject: science
question: Which body part do we use to hear?
explanation: We use our ears to hear! Our ears catch sounds from all around us.

subject: science
question: What is a baby cow called?
explanation: A baby cow is called a calf! Calves drink milk from their mothers.

subject: science
question: What does ice turn into when it melts?
explanation: Ice turns into water when it melts! Heat makes the ice change from solid to liquid.

subject: science
question: How many legs does an insect have?
explanation: Insects have 6 legs! Ants, bees, and butterflies are all insects with 6 legs.

subject: science
question: Which season comes after winter?
explanation: Spring comes after winter! In spring, flowers bloom and baby animals are born!

subject: science
question: What do cows give us?
explanation: Cows give us milk! You might drink milk or eat cheese made from milk.

subject: science
question: Which body part pumps blood through your body?
explanation: Your heart pumps blood! It beats all day and all night to keep you alive. You can feel it in your chest!

subject: science
question: What does a caterpillar turn into?
explanation: A caterpillar turns into a butterfly! It wraps itself in a cocoon and comes out with beautiful wings!

subject: science
question: The Earth goes around the ___.
explanation: The Earth goes around the Sun! It takes one whole year to go all the way around.

subject: science
question: Which animal gives us eggs?
explanation: Hens give us eggs! A hen is a female chicken that lays eggs.

subject: science
question: What do we use our tongue for?
explanation: We use our tongue for tasting! Your tongue can taste sweet, sour, salty, and bitter things!

subject: science
question: What is a baby sheep called?
explanation: A baby sheep is called a lamb! Lambs have soft, fluffy wool.

subject: science
question: What keeps us warm when it is cold outside?
explanation: Warm clothes keep us warm! Things like jackets, scarves, and gloves trap our body heat.

subject: science
question: Which animal can fly?
explanation: Birds can fly! They have wings that help them soar through the sky.

subject: science
question: What do we see in the sky at night?
explanation: We see the moon and stars at night! They twinkle and shine in the dark sky.

subject: science
question: Which part of the plant makes food?
explanation: Leaves make food for the plant! They use sunlight, water, and air to make food. How amazing!

subject: science
question: What is a baby frog called?
explanation: A baby frog is called a tadpole! Tadpoles live in water and slowly grow legs to become frogs!

subject: science
question: How many senses do humans have?
explanation: We have 5 senses: sight, hearing, smell, taste, and touch! They help us understand the world.

subject: science
question: What is the color of most leaves?
explanation: Most leaves are green! They get their green color from something called chlorophyll.

subject: science
question: Which animal has a trunk?
explanation: Elephants have trunks! They use their trunks to drink water, grab food, and even say hello!

subject: science
question: What season is the hottest?
explanation: Summer is the hottest season! It is when we go swimming and eat ice cream!

subject: science
question: What do bees make?
explanation: Bees make honey! They collect nectar from flowers and turn it into sweet, yummy honey.

subject: science
question: Which body part helps us think?
explanation: Our brain helps us think! It is inside our head and controls everything we do.

subject: science
question: What is a baby horse called?
explanation: A baby horse is called a foal! Foals can stand up and walk very soon after they are born.

subject: science
question: What do fish use to breathe?
explanation: Fish breathe through gills! Gills take oxygen from the water. That is why fish can live underwater!

subject: science
question: What do we call frozen water?
explanation: Frozen water is called ice! When water gets very cold, it freezes and becomes hard ice.

subject: science
question: Which season do we see snow?
explanation: We see snow in winter! Snow falls when it is very cold outside.

subject: science
question: What is the sense of touch used for?
explanation: Touch helps us feel things! We can feel if something is soft, rough, hot, or cold.

subject: science
question: What animal is known as man's best friend?
explanation: Dogs are called man's best friend! They are loyal and loving pets.

subject: science
question: What do seeds grow into?
explanation: Seeds grow into plants! A tiny seed can grow into a big, strong plant with enough water and sunshine.

subject: science
question: What gives us light during the day?
explanation: The Sun gives us light during the day! It is a big, bright star that keeps our planet warm.

subject: science
question: Which animal has stripes?
explanation: Zebras have black and white stripes! Every zebra has a unique pattern of stripes.

subject: science
question: Where does a fish live?
explanation: Fish live in water! They can live in rivers, lakes, and oceans.

subject: science
question: What is a baby duck called?
explanation: A baby duck is called a duckling! Ducklings follow their mother in a line. So cute!

subject: science
question: What is the hard part inside your body that helps you stand?
explanation: Bones help you stand and move! Your body has over 200 bones that make up your skeleton!

subject: science
question: What appears in the sky after rain and sunshine?
explanation: A rainbow appears after rain when the sun shines! It has 7 beautiful colors!

subject: science
question: Which animal gives us wool?
explanation: Sheep give us wool! Their fluffy coat is sheared and made into warm clothes.

subject: science
question: What do lungs help us do?
explanation: Lungs help us breathe! We have two lungs that fill with air every time we breathe in.
